name: Thread language consistently across serde (resolve lang vs language)
case_description: Other inconsistency. M1 covered the 7 new threading variants with `language: Option<Language>` + `alias = "lang"`. The pre-existing Structure variant (types.rs:358) used `lang: Option<String>` as its canonical wire field name, with NO acceptance of the `language` form — creating exactly the wire-format split the V-bundle flagged.

tdd.red_failure_summary: Case 3 (Structure + canonical `language` key): assertion left=None right=Some("rust"). Pre-fix Structure rejects the canonical wire form because its `lang: Option<String>` field has no `alias = "language"`; the canonical key is silently dropped and the field stays None.
tdd.regression_test_locks: v0.2.x clients sending `lang` continue to deserialize on BOTH Structure (where `lang` was the canonical name) and Calls (where `lang` is the alias). Locks the alias surface indefinitely.

green_impl_shape: Replaced `Structure { path: PathBuf, lang: Option<String> }` (single-line struct-pattern variant) with a multi-line struct variant whose `lang: Option<String>` field carries `#[serde(default, rename = "language", alias = "lang", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The Rust field name remains `lang` so the two daemon.rs callsites (line 574 destructure, line 1529 construction) and spec.md are NOT touched (they are in cluster-M2's wave-2 scope, not M4's). On the wire the canonical form becomes `"language"` (matching the 7 M1-threaded variants); the legacy `"lang"` form is accepted via alias.

daemon_test.result: 33 passed; 0 failed; 42 ignored (warm + cmd subprocess tests, ignored independent of M4)

workspace_build.result: Finished `dev` profile, no errors, no warnings on M4 changes

warm_variant_note: Did NOT modify the Warm variant (types.rs:329) although it also has `language: Option<String>` without an `alias = "lang"`. The contract green_impl_shape explicitly enumerates `Structure plus the 7 added in M1` as the 8 variants in scope; Warm is not in that list. Touching Warm here would silently expand scope. If the Warm variant should also accept `lang`, that is a follow-up scoped to a separate milestone — out of M4's stop_threshold.
